changes_made: Renamed k8s_pod_security_policy_vulnerability_patching to k8s_pod_security_vulnerability_patching for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to flaw remediation. The naming mostly follows conventions, but a minor adjustment was made for consistency. The functions address vulnerability scanning and patching, which are key aspects of flaw remediation in Kubernetes.
naming_improvements: Ensure consistent use of resource names, such as using 'pod_security' instead of 'pod_security_policy' when referring to general pod security practices.
title: Flaw Remediation - Incorporate flaw remediation into the...
step1_reasoning: The control SI-2-d focuses on flaw remediation, which in Kubernetes can be addressed through mechanisms that ensure vulnerabilities are identified and remediated. This includes admission controllers that enforce vulnerability scanning on images, ensuring images are scanned before deployment, and applying security patches. Additionally, ensuring nodes are automatically updated helps in remediating flaws at the infrastructure level.
